blurb: Soft, plump leaves with translucent 'windowed' tips and fine bristly edges — in the wild it grows half-buried, letting light down through those windows. A gentle-natured, thirstier Haworthia.

care.light: Bright but indirect light — those soft windowed leaves burn in direct sun. Filtered light keeps them plump and translucent rather than red-stressed and shrivelled.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, but it likes a touch more water than the hard-leaved haworthias; when the leaves start to look less plump, give it a good drink and let it drain.
care.soil: A gritty mix with a little more moisture retention than a pure cactus blend; still must drain freely.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~40°F and out of frost.
care.dormancy: Opportunistic — grows in the mild months and rests in peak heat and cold; water less then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice through the growing season.
care.potting: Repot every couple of years to refresh the mix and separate the clump.
care.propagation: Offsets pull away with a few roots and pot up easily.
care.bloom: Thin stalks of small white tubular flowers.
care.troubles: Overwatering (mushy, translucent-to-brown leaves) and too much sun (reddening, scorch); mealybugs occasionally.
